title: How can we run chef inspec against podmain container
body:
Hello,

**Please guide me if I am posting this issue in wrong place.**

I am running podman container inside my base container (customised ubuntu 16.04) with --privileged flag. When I am trying to run chef inspec against my podman container using below commands it gives me following error. 
```
# podman run -d docker.io/saran07/python3-ubuntu16.04 /bin/sleep 10000s
# podman ps
# inspec exec cis-ubuntu-controls -t docker://containerid --chef-license accept
```
```
podman version is 1.4.3
inspec version is 4.7.3
```
Errors : 
```
+---------------------------------------------+
✔ 1 product license accepted.
+---------------------------------------------+
```

comments.body: it looks to me like inspec doesnt understand podman?  as you say, it is looking for a docker socket to get information.  unfortunately that will not work with a podman container.
shoiuld we close this issue in favor of following the upstream request?
Yes I think this is more about chef then podman.
